CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-07-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Continued To Aug-26-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Change Venue; Request For Costs And Fees; Marin Healthcare Districts Motion To Change Venue To Marin County; Request For Costs And Fees
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS MARIN HEALTHCARE DISTRICT, AND MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL'S Motion To Change Venue; Request For Costs And Fees is taken off calendar as duplicate entry.

Amended Notice Of Motion To Change Venue; Request For Costs And Fees; Mrin Healthcare Districts Motion To Change Venue To Marin County; Request For Costs And Fees
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS MARIN HEALTHCARE DISTRICT, AND MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL'S Amended Motion To Change Venue; Request For Costs And Fees is Granted. This action is transferred to Marin County. Marin Healthcare District is a public entity as a matter of law. Gov. Code 7530 provides that "districts" are public entities for all purposes. Further, the Health & Safety Code governs healthcare districts, and provides that they are established by the approval of district's voters, they are supported by taxes from residents in the district, and their governing boards are elected by district residents. (Health & Safety Code, 32000 et seq.) The Health & Safety Code also provides that in order to make a claim against a healthcare district, the Government Claim Act procedures must be followed. (Id., 32492.) Additionally, the complaint alleges that it served a "timely government claim." (Complaint, par. 4.) Although Plaintiff now argues that it "prophylactically filed claims" just in case Marin Healthcare District was a public agency, there is nothing in the complaint that indicates that Plaintiff questioned Marin Healthcare District's public entity status. Marin Healthcare District also submits the Declaration of Leo Domanico in reply, which establishes that Marin Healthcare District is a public entity. (Declaration of Leo Domanico, par. 2.) Plaintiff may respond to this evidence orally at the hearing if she chooses to do so. Pursuant to CCP sec. 396(b), Plaintiff shall pay Defendants reasonable attorney's fees and costs in the amount of $540. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
